title: rubia fruticosa subsp. pendula
intro: Rubia fruticosa subsp. pendula, commonly known as the 'Pendulous Madder', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of anthraquinones, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubia fruticosa subsp. pendula include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Rubia fruticosa subsp. pendula is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and menstrual disorders.
